page: Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is a Cloud Compute virtual machine configured with 6 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance utilizes an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz, structured as 3 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled (2 threads per core). The CPU features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 4 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instructions. Storage is bundled as a 320 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear trade-off between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The shared CPU allocation results in weak single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1272.09 and a PassMark CPU score of 6458.84. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core reaching 6774.42. Memory read bandwidth is moderate, measuring 407.68 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 48.31 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weak at 18.72 GB/sec.

Computational workloads requiring heavy single-threaded throughput, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, show poor performance. Database operations and static web serving are also constrained, with PassMark database operations scoring in the weak tier and static web serving throughput limited to 5.39 GB/sec. Conversely, the instance handles Redis SET operations efficiently, achieving an average tier result of 6.15 million operations per second. Small LLM text generation also performs at an average level of 97.28 tokens per second, though larger models like the 7B parameter model are highly restricted.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the vc2-6c-16gb offers a high density of vCPUs, memory, and NVMe storage for budget-conscious deployments. Because the CPU resources are shared, it is best suited for workloads that experience intermittent activity rather than sustained, high-intensity processing. Appropriate use cases include development environments, low-traffic web applications, background worker queues, and lightweight Redis caching layers.
description: Vultr vc2-6c-16gb is a budget-friendly Cloud Compute instance featuring 6 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance is designed for workloads that benefit from multi-core capacity without requiring sustained, dedicated CPU performance. Benchmark data reveals a trade-off: single-core performance and database operations are weak, while multi-core stress tests and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. With 2.67 GB of memory per core and a shared CPU model, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense but non-critical workloads. It is best suited for development environments, lightweight caching, and low-traffic web applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining six shared vCPUs, sixteen gigabytes of memory, and local NVMe storage for non-critical workloads.

320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Average multi-core and Redis SET performance
Weak single-core and database execution profiles
Suitable for development, caching, and low-traffic web serving
